Fungal infection.desc: Skin infection caused by fungi affecting skin, nails, or scalp.

Allergy.desc: Immune reaction to foreign substances like pollen, food, or insect stings.

GERD.desc: Stomach acid frequently flows back into the esophagus causing irritation.

Chronic cholestasis.desc: Liver condition where bile flow is reduced or blocked.

Drug Reaction.desc: Adverse reaction to medication ranging from mild rash to severe anaphylaxis.

Peptic ulcer diseae.desc: Sores on the lining of the stomach or upper small intestine.

AIDS.desc: Advanced HIV infection severely damaging the immune system.

Diabetes.desc: Metabolic disease causing high blood sugar due to insulin issues.

Gastroenteritis.desc: Inflammation of stomach and intestines causing vomiting and diarrhea.

Bronchial Asthma.desc: Chronic condition where airways narrow and produce extra mucus.

Hypertension.desc: Persistently high blood pressure leading to heart disease risk.

Migraine.desc: Neurological condition causing intense headaches with nausea.

Cervical spondylosis.desc: Age-related wear on spinal disks in the neck causing pain.

Paralysis (brain hemorrhage).desc: Loss of muscle function caused by bleeding in the brain.

Jaundice.desc: Yellowing of skin and eyes due to excess bilirubin in blood.

Malaria.desc: Mosquito-borne disease caused by Plasmodium parasites.

Chicken pox.desc: Highly contagious viral infection causing itchy blister-like rash.

Dengue.desc: Mosquito-borne viral infection causing high fever and rash.

Typhoid.desc: Bacterial infection caused by Salmonella typhi via contaminated food.

hepatitis A.desc: Liver infection caused by Hepatitis A virus via contaminated food.

Hepatitis B.desc: Serious liver infection caused by Hepatitis B virus.

Hepatitis C.desc: Viral infection causing liver inflammation and possible liver damage.

Hepatitis D.desc: Liver disease only occurring alongside Hepatitis B infection.

Hepatitis E.desc: Liver disease caused by Hepatitis E virus via contaminated water.

Alcoholic hepatitis.desc: Liver inflammation caused by excessive alcohol consumption.

Tuberculosis.desc: Serious bacterial infection mainly affecting the lungs.

Common Cold.desc: Viral infection of the upper respiratory tract.

Pneumonia.desc: Infection inflaming air sacs in one or both lungs.

Dimorphic hemmorhoids(piles).desc: Swollen veins in rectum causing pain and bleeding.

Heart attack.desc: Blockage of blood flow to heart muscle — medical emergency.

Varicose veins.desc: Enlarged twisted veins appearing in legs due to faulty valves.

Hypothyroidism.desc: Underactive thyroid failing to produce enough thyroid hormone.

Hyperthyroidism.desc: Overactive thyroid producing too much thyroxine.

Hypoglycemia.desc: Abnormally low blood sugar causing shakiness and confusion.

Osteoarthritis.desc: Degeneration of joint cartilage causing pain and stiffness.

Arthritis.desc: Inflammation of joints causing pain and reduced movement.

(vertigo) Paroxysmal Positional Vertigo.desc: Inner ear disorder causing brief episodes of dizziness.

Acne.desc: Skin condition causing pimples and blackheads on face and body.

Urinary tract infection.desc: Infection in any part of the urinary system, commonly the bladder.

Psoriasis.desc: Skin disease causing red itchy scaly patches.

Impetigo.desc: Highly contagious bacterial skin infection causing red sores.
